before_behavior.tracked_assets: could remain {} indefinitely even when open positions existed
before_behavior.stream_subscription: subscribed to nothing and did not automatically recover when discovery/token mapping later became available

after_behavior.tracked_assets: recomputed when open positions exist and current tracked asset mapping is empty
after_behavior.discovery_refresh: dashboard_server now triggers a safe discovery refresh in that specific recovery case
after_behavior.resubscribe: market_stream now forces websocket reconnect when tracked asset set changes, causing a fresh subscribe payload to be sent

exact_trigger_recompute_resubscribe_path.trigger: open positions exist AND _tracked_open_assets() returns empty
exact_trigger_recompute_resubscribe_path.resubscribe: GLOBAL_MARKET_STREAM.update_tracked_assets(tracked) returns changed state and closes current websocket so run loop reconnects with new assets_ids payload

proof.before: diagnostic step previously observed _tracked_open_assets() == {} in live runtime
proof.after: after restart with fix, _tracked_open_assets() returned non-empty tracked asset mappings for live open positions
proof.example: live runtime now resolves tracked slugs like mlb-phi-sf-2026-04-08 to asset IDs instead of staying empty forever

sse_status.notes: fallback remains intact; this fix removes the zero-asset deadlock but does not by itself prove live mark ingress has fully started

truthfulness_note: This task fixes the subscription-tracking deadlock. It does not by itself claim full Stage 1 completion until true live stream marks are observed.
